subject: PM-Mapper CSIT image pulls enhancements
commit_message:
PM-Mapper CSIT image pulls enhancements

- Add nexus3.onap.org repository to external images
to avoid dockerhub pull limits.
- Remove repository from PM-Mapper image to force use
of image built earlier with maven.
.gitignore update.
- Add DMaaP healthcheck to setup.sh scripts

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2796
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2799
Change-Id: Ie98336b91b37d61767049d4857b016fb618e4e8e
Signed-off-by: Remigiusz Janeczek <remigiusz.janeczek@nokia.com>

This was an interesting result; I hit one like this *once* when I was running these locally:

 Pm-mapper drop: 0 messages. Expected drop count: 6-9

...only in my case there were 4 dropped messages. There seems to be some randomness in this test case?

Patch Set 10: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 10:

My guess was that the random fails occured because datarouter containers didn't fully start before sending requests to them (no indication in available logs though, I was only able to find that there are no logs for publish  to PM-Mapper in DR, like there was no subscription. Adding sleeps to setup.sh scripts seems to solve the issue)

Patch Set 10:

Yes, you're trying to address DCAEGEN2-2799 (rather than DCAEGEN2-2796) with this change, so that should be perhaps handled in a separate commit anyway. 

Is it the case that the first four tests in pmmapper suite do not need DMaaP (as they passed in, for example, https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-services-pm-mapper-review-verification-maven-master/11/consoleFull)?

Patch Set 10:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 10:
> 
> > Adding sleeps to setup.sh scripts seems to solve the issue
> 
> Can you replicate the failure somewhere *without* the sleep?

I wasn't able to replicate the issue locally. Jenkins job run on patchset 7 were ran without the sleep.


> Patch Set 10:
> 
> Yes, you're trying to address DCAEGEN2-2799 (rather than DCAEGEN2-2796) with this change, so that should be perhaps handled in a separate commit anyway. 
> 
> Is it the case that the first four tests in pmmapper suite do not need DMaaP (as they passed in, for example, https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-services-pm-mapper-review-verification-maven-master/11/consoleFull)?

Yes, that's exactly the case. Moreover fourth test from the pmmapper suite actually checks if pmmapper file processing works fine, by directly sending a file to the same endpoint that DMaaP would query.

Patch Set 10: Code-Review+1

+1'ing this as these are my very last minutes with ONAP project for now, so I won't have my downvote blocking your progress further
